data.narrative:
Newcleo is a Series B nuclear energy company that has raised €537M to date across its founding team of Stefano Buono, Luciano Cinotti, and Elisabeth Rizzotti. The valuation universe comprises eight private peers in nuclear energy—including Type One Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power—alongside three public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR), though the broader sector remains concentrated with limited public liquidity.

Peer-median analysis is not applicable to this valuation. The nuclear energy sector contains only one same-stage comparable and one adjacent-stage peer, falling short of the three-datapoint minimum required for reliable median estimation. Expanding the filter to capture all eight same-sector private peers introduces heterogeneity across funding stages and valuation tiers that would obscure rather than inform valuation. Public comps provide market-based reference points but face materiality gaps given Newcleo's pre-revenue development stage relative to more mature nuclear operators.

The thinness of narrow peer data prevents triangulation to a single range at this stage. Any valuation conclusion must acknowledge that the nuclear energy sector's limited comparable universe—particularly at Series B—creates material estimation uncertainty. Subsequent analysis would require either scenario-based approaches anchored to development milestones and capital requirements, or acceptance of a wide range reflecting high dispersion across the available comp set. The €537M already raised provides a floor against which any valuation should be benchmarked.
